key_insight: Meta-RL can tune ICCBF class-K functions to produce safer, less fuel-hungry spacecraft proximity controllers, and Mamba + PPO performed best in the paper’s tests.
engineering_relevance: This work suggests a practical path to adaptive, thrust-constrained safety control for autonomous rendezvous and proximity operations, including adversarial scenarios.
method: Compare LSTM, GRU, and Mamba recurrent architectures for meta-RL tuning of ICCBF recursion.
Train with PPO or SAC to learn class-K functions that shape the safe control set.
Evaluate cooperative and adversarial target behaviors for safety, task completion, and fuel use.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation across cooperative and uncooperative RPO scenarios, with Mamba trained by PPO yielding superior task completion, safety, and fuel savings.
watch_item: The reported gains depend on simulation/test scenarios, so real spacecraft deployment may still face model mismatch, tuning sensitivity, and verification challenges.
paper_title: Memory-Efficient Meta-Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Safety-Critical Control in Adversarial Spacecraft Proximity Operations
